costs: Cost of designing, piloting, and validating new admissions instruments
Subsidized preparation-access programs require sustained funding
Risk of only marginal effect since track structure and its underlying stratification incentives remain intact
content.equity_impact: Modest positive impact plausible: reduces one specific channel of SES-linked selection (private preparation advantage) but leaves the structural three-stage funnel and its known segregation effects largely intact [F-live-portuguese_reform-01, F-live-hungarian_education_system-03]. Unlikely to substantially close the 53-point socioeconomic achievement gap on its own [F-live-portuguese_reform-03].
expected_benefits: Directly targets the documented SES-selection-independent-of-ability mechanism without full structural upheaval [F-live-education_finance-03]

Politically more feasible than abolition given explicit ministerial disavowal of abolition and stated preference for admissions-focused reform [F-live-legal_and_governance-06, F-live-implementation_planning-06]
Avoids transition costs and disruption associated with structural reform (school closures, placement crises) seen in comparator reversals [F-live-polish_reform-05]
content.goal: Reduce socioeconomic selection bias in gimnázium admissions by reforming the entrance-exam and application process, without abolishing the 6/8-year tracks themselves.

implementation_steps.action: Launch public consultation on admissions criteria, per Minister Lannert's call for societal dialogue
Pilot revised weighting (e.g., reduced reliance on private-prep-sensitive exam components, increased weight on in-school teacher assessment) in a subset of schools
Fund or subsidize free public exam-preparation resources to offset private tutoring advantage

mechanisms: Selection bias operates via prior test-score-independent SES effects and private exam preparation access, both of which are admissions-process features rather than structural track features [F-live-education_finance-03, F-live-education_finance-06]

Self-exclusion by disadvantaged families is driven by decisions to enter/remain in preparation processes rather than the exams themselves, suggesting preparation-access interventions could shift behavior [F-live-legal_and_governance-05]
Minister's stated position frames admissions reform as the primary lever under consideration, distinct from structural abolition [F-live-polish_reform-06, F-live-legal_and_governance-06]
risks: Even narrowly framed admissions review has already triggered partisan conflict, with opposition framing it as disguised structural transformation [F-live-implementation_planning-06, F-live-political_feasibility-04]

Self-exclusion is reportedly driven by parental strategic decisions rather than exam design itself, suggesting admissions reform alone may have limited effect on actual segregation [F-live-legal_and_governance-05]
Church and private maintainers operate outside Klebelsberg Centre authority, limiting the reach of any centrally mandated admissions redesign [F-live-legal_and_governance-03]
content.title: Reform admissions process while preserving track structure
